subject: Computer Networking
title: Social Engineering
description: Learn Social Engineering in one minute.

hook.text: The strongest firewall in the world can't stop a human who clicks a bad link. People are the weakest point.

buildup.text: Social engineering tricks people into giving up passwords, access, or money through manipulation, not hacking.

discovery.text: Phishing emails look like they're from your bank. Pretexting calls sound like IT support. The disguises are convincing.

twist.text: Even tech companies fall for it. In 2020, a teen tricked Twitter employees into giving access to celebrity accounts.

climax.text: Training and awareness are the only defenses. Technology can flag suspicious messages, but judgment is the last line.

punchline.text: Hackers don't always hack computers. Sometimes they hack people.

quiz.question: What is social engineering in cybersecurity?
options: Manipulating people into revealing sensitive information
